run.coverageWindow: 2026-07-17 morning PDT through 2026-07-20 morning PDT, with weekend discovery because the run is Monday

tierChecks.contract: Daily full scan of new items
tierChecks.notes: APTN, NationTalk/BC NationTalk search paths, Indigenous Business & Finance Today, CCIB, FNFA, FNFMB, FNMPC, Indigenous Energy Monitor, Indigenous Clean Energy, BCAFN economic-development path, Indigenous Tourism BC, First Peoples Law, Nunatsiaq, Indigenous Watchdog, Ha-Shilth-Sa, FNTC and NACCA were checked through direct opens or targeted search. The included fresh signals were RBT2/Major Projects Office plus Tsawwassen First Nation's response, Treaty No. 6/APTN referendum-rights reporting, Ontario wildfire/Alert Ready reporting, and B.C. First Nations nuclear exploration.

tierChecks.contract: Daily change detection across long-range analysis and specialist newsletters
tierChecks.notes: Yellowhead Institute, JFK Law, RRII, Tallgrass/IIPWG, FNTAA, Indigenous Finance Collective, Raven, AFOA/JAM, NATOA, Indigenomics, Tulo, FNIGC, ILI, OKT and Osler were change-checked. First Peoples Law's July 8-14 report remained the latest visible legal roundup and was retained only as discovery/ledger context.

tierChecks.contract: Substacks and interpretive newsletters for discovery only
tierChecks.notes: Nuanced, Adam Olsen, MEDIA INDIGENA, Indigenous Insider, Lotusland, Indigenous Wire and Indigenomics in ACTION were change-checked through public search paths. No material claim in the edition relies on commentary or Substack sources.

tierChecks.contract: Regional and community journalism change detection
tierChecks.notes: Anishinabek News, Eagle Feather News, The Eastern Door, Nation Magazine, Cabin Radio, Ku'ku'kwes News and Ha-Shilth-Sa/IndigiNews regional paths were checked. Regional material either did not clear the economic-materiality bar or was superseded by stronger primary/independent sources.

tierChecks.contract: Friday long-form rotation only unless event-driven
tierChecks.notes: No Friday Tier E deep-read rotation was required on this Monday run. Long Current was included only because the live B.C. nuclear exploration item connected to an active Indigenous equity-and-loan-guarantee structure in Ontario.

tierChecks.contract: Search label 'Nations Brief Sources' since prior run
tierChecks.notes: Gmail search returned no messages for label:"Nations Brief Sources" after:2026/7/17 -in:spam -in:trash.

candidateLedger.candidate: Roberts Bank Terminal 2 named in Canada's Port of Vancouver Gateway Strategy and Major Projects Office review path
candidateLedger.verification: Federal government release plus Tsawwassen First Nation statement. Framed as review/strategy and conditional Nation response, not project approval.

candidateLedger.candidate: Treaty No. 6 emergency resolution and Quebec First Nations condemnation of Alberta referendum/separation rhetoric
candidateLedger.verification: Treaty organization statement plus APTN reporting; AFN final resolution package not yet visible, so item is labelled reported.

candidateLedger.candidate: Ontario wildfire evacuation, Alert Ready expansion and federal assistance
candidateLedger.verification: Canadian Press reporting through CityNews plus Public Safety Canada federal-assistance release. Community-specific rebuild/funding details remain unknown.

candidateLedger.candidate: B.C. First Nations exploration of nuclear energy and SMRs
candidateLedger.verification: Global News reporting for the B.C. exploratory visit; Ontario government release and Major Projects Office record for the Darlington Indigenous equity/loan-guarantee model. Framed as exploration, not a B.C. project.
Included July 17; no material new assessment filing or consent update found in weekend scan.
Included July 17; no material new implementation, licensing, production or economics disclosure found in weekend scan.

candidateLedger.candidate: Tsleil-Waututh Nation Burrard Inlet dredging challenge
candidateLedger.verification: No new court filing or public project update found in weekend scan.

candidateLedger.candidate: Mount Polley tailings-dam approval over Xat'sull First Nation concerns
candidateLedger.verification: No new permit step or public response found in weekend scan.
Checked as Inuit/northern economic file; no new July 17-20 court, monitor, Qikiqtani Inuit Association or company development found strong enough to update the ledger.

candidateLedger.candidate: LNG Canada Phase 2 storage-tank equity option
candidateLedger.verification: No new financing, FID, lease-economics or Nation/company disclosure appeared in the weekend scan.

sourceFailuresAndGaps: NationTalk and BC NationTalk were not reliably fetchable through direct open paths; targeted search paths were used and the limitation is recorded rather than using NationTalk as source foundation.
The final AFN 2026 annual general assembly resolution package was not visible during the scan, so the Alberta referendum item is framed through Treaty No. 6 and APTN and labelled reported.

Some Global News article/video text was available only through search/open metadata. The B.C. nuclear item is therefore limited to the exploratory visit and is verified structurally against Ontario primary records, not framed as a B.C. transaction.
No fresh Inuit or Métis economic, ownership, capital, procurement, fiscal-powers or regulatory item cleared both materiality and verification in the weekend window.

qualityGates.twoSourceVerificationForConsequentialCla: Applied to RBT2, referendum-rights, wildfire and nuclear items through primary statements, government releases and independent reporting. Items with incomplete primary records are labelled reported.
qualityGates.distinctionsBasedLanguage: First Nations-specific developments are not generalized to Inuit or Métis. The absence of Inuit/Métis placements is disclosed in Still Watching.
qualityGates.completedVsProposed: RBT2 is described as a review/strategy file, not a completed approval. B.C. nuclear is described as exploratory, not a project. Darlington is used only as an Ontario model with published loan-guarantee/equity information.
qualityGates.bodySourceChips: None intended; numbered references are used and full descriptive source list is placed at the bottom.
qualityGates.dateIntegrity: Fresh included items are July 16-19, 2026 and are included because the Monday run covers the weekend since the prior July 17 run. Older sources are explicitly ledger or structural context.
qualityGates.priorEditionDeduplication: July 17 items were not repeated as news unless they had new context. Yellowhead, Gitanmaax, Burrard and Mount Polley appear only in the B.C. ledger with no material-change claim.
